commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=40b779bd5662330a38e73d4118c3f4a6d28da06b)
commands.review_summary:
Adds an additive `agents.setDefault` Gateway RPC across protocol schemas, core descriptors, SDK, docs, Swift models, config mutation helpers, handler code, and tests.

PR surface: Source +113, Tests +118, Docs +1, Other +32. Total +264 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows the PR adds the handler and descriptor but not the lazy `coreGatewayHandlers` registration, so full Gateway dispatch would not reach the new handler.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The code blocker is narrow, but the external PR also needs contributor-supplied real Gateway/SDK proof after the dispatch fix, so this should stay in normal review rather than a repair lane.

target.title: feat(gateway): add agents.setDefault RPC method
target.body:
## Summary

There is no dedicated Gateway RPC to change the default agent. `agents.list` already returns a read-only `defaultId` and full CRUD exists (`agents.create`/`agents.update`/`agents.delete`), but `agents.update` does not accept a `default` field, so the only way to flip the default is the generic `config.patch` (ADMIN): read the whole config, find the agent's index, build a merge-patch, and reconcile `baseHash`. A "set as default" button should not have to reimplement config surgery (openclaw#52928).

This adds `agents.setDefault` (`{ agentId } -> { ok: true, defaultId }`, scope `operator.admin`). It marks the target as default, clears the flag from every other entry, persists through the same retrying config-write path the other `agents.*` mutations use, and rejects unknown agent ids.

## Why a dedicated method

The default agent is a per-entry `default?: boolean` flag on `agents.list[]` (`src/config/types.agents.ts:82`), resolved by `resolveDefaultAgentId` (`src/agents/agent-scope-config.ts:91`), which takes the single entry with `default: true` and falls back to the first entry. Nothing exposed a write path for that flag:

- `agents.update`'s param schema is `additionalProperties: false` with no `default` field, so passing `default: true` is rejected.
- `applyAgentConfig` (`src/commands/agents.config.ts`) merges `name`/`workspace`/`model`/`identity` and never touches `default`.

So clients were pushed onto `config.patch`, which is exactly the index-lookup + merge-patch + `baseHash` dance the issue calls out.

## Implementation

The handler mirrors the existing `agents.update`/`agents.delete` shape, so the new surface reuses the same validation, precondition, and retrying-write plumbing rather than inventing a parallel one:

- `setDefaultAgent` (`src/commands/agents.config.ts`): pure transform next to `applyAgentConfig`/`pruneAgentConfig`. Sets `default: true` on the target and `false` on the rest, leaving unchanged entries referentially identical so the persisted config diff stays minimal.
- `setDefaultAgentConfigEntry` (`agents-config-mutations.ts`): wraps `setDefaultAgent` in `mutateConfigFileWithRetry`, throws `AgentConfigPreconditionError("not-found", id)` for unknown ids, and returns the resolved `defaultId`.
- `agents.setDefault` handler (`server-methods/agents.ts`): `validateAgentsSetDefaultParams` -> `isConfiguredAgent` guard -> mutation -> `{ ok: true, defaultId }`.
- Descriptor `{ name: "agents.setDefault", scope: "operator.admin" }` (`core-descriptors.ts`), same scope as the other agent mutations.

## Surfaces covered

One chokepoint per layer, so the method is fully wired and not half-exposed:

- Protocol: `AgentsSetDefaultParams`/`AgentsSetDefaultResult` schemas + types + validator + re-exports (`packages/gateway-protocol/src/...`), and regenerated artifacts (`dist/protocol.schema.json` via `protocol:gen`; tracked Swift `GatewayModels.swift` via `protocol:gen:swift`, +32 additive).
- SDK: `oc.agents.setDefault({ agentId })` (`packages/sdk/src/client.ts`).
- Docs: `docs/gateway/protocol.md` agent-helpers list.

No new config keys, no migration, no change to `resolveDefaultAgentId`, and `agents.update` keeps its existing field set.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/agents.config.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agents-mutate.test.ts src/gateway/methods/registry.test.ts` -> pass. New: pure `setDefaultAgent` transform (target/clear, normalize, idempotent identity, multi-default collapse) and the `agents.setDefault` handler (success + persisted flip, unknown-agent rejection with no write, malformed-params rejection, idempotent re-set).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/gateway-protocol` -> 210 passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental` -> clean.
- `oxfmt --check` + `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on all touched files -> clean.
- Full `pnpm build` not run on this box (memory-constrained); protocol artifacts were regenerated and the Swift diff is purely additive.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: operator/SDK clients can now flip the default agent with one `agents.setDefault` call that persists to config, instead of being pushed onto `config.patch`; unknown ids are rejected without mutating config.

Real environment tested: a throwaway harness (not committed) drove the real production `agentsHandlers["agents.setDefault"]` against a real on-disk `openclaw.json` (via `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`), using the real `loadConfig`, the real `mutateConfigFileWithRetry` writer, and the real `resolveDefaultAgentId`. No mocks.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: seeded a config with agents `main` (default), `research`, `ops`; called the handler with `{ agentId: "research" }`, then re-read the file and re-resolved the default; then called it with an unknown id `ghost`; then re-set the current default to prove idempotency.

Evidence after fix:

```
=== BEFORE ===
handler registered now: true
config default flags: [{"id":"main","default":true},{"id":"research","default":false},{"id":"ops","default":false}]
resolveDefaultAgentId: main

=== CALL agents.setDefault { agentId: 'research' } ===
response: {"ok":true,"result":{"ok":true,"defaultId":"research"}}

=== AFTER (persisted to disk) ===
config default flags: [{"id":"main","default":false},{"id":"research","default":true},{"id":"ops","default":false}]
resolveDefaultAgentId: research

=== reject unknown agent ===
response: {"ok":false,"error":{"code":"INVALID_REQUEST","message":"agent \"ghost\" not found"}}
config default flags unchanged: [{"id":"main","default":false},{"id":"research","default":true},{"id":"ops","default":false}]

=== idempotent re-set of current default ===
response: {"ok":true,"result":{"ok":true,"defaultId":"research"}}
config default flags: [{"id":"main","default":false},{"id":"research","default":true},{"id":"ops","default":false}]
```

Observed result after fix: `setDefault research` flips `research` to the sole `default: true` entry on disk and `resolveDefaultAgentId` returns `research`; an unknown id returns an `INVALID_REQUEST` "not found" error and leaves the config untouched; re-setting the current default is a no-op that still returns the right `defaultId`.

What was not tested: a live WebSocket round-trip from a third-party SDK consumer outside the repo; this change is the Gateway RPC + config-mutation layer and is covered by the unit/handler tests plus the real-handler, real-config before/after harness above.

## References

- Closes #52928.
- Sibling mutations `agents.create`/`agents.update`/`agents.delete` in `src/gateway/server-methods/agents.ts` (same scope and plumbing).
